chunks.section: Placement Cell Overview — Mission, Objectives & Placement Committee
chunks.context: This chunk describes the overall mission and objectives of the MSAJCE Placement Cell and lists the full placement committee with names and positions.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Placement Cell Overview: In a competitive job market, employers expect graduates to have a range of skills and right experience of the workplace. MSAJCE provides at most care, support and ample opportunities to help students develop requisite skills and expertise and make them employable graduates. Placement Cell Objectives: (1) To provide Employment Opportunities for students in reputed companies. (2) To organize On and Off Campus Recruitment Drives for students. (3) To Train students on employability skills such as Aptitude Skills, Communication Skills and Technical Skills. (4) To conduct Value Added Certification courses to gain domain specific knowledge. (5) To conduct Mock Aptitude, Mock Interview, Mock GD to prepare students to face the interview with confidence. (6) To arrange In-plant training, Internship and Industrial visits. (7) To organize workshops on Recent Technologies by industrial experts which benefits students to understand technological innovations. Besides providing support to entrepreneurial ventures, MSAJCE keeps its employability services up to date with the demands of the job market and aims to ensure that graduates are in the strongest position possible when they leave. Placement Committee: S.No. | Name | Position: 1 | Mr. S.V. Vinodh | Placement Officer. 2 | Mr. Ajin Sijo John | Assistant Placement Officer. 3 | Mrs. N. Kavitha | Member. 4 | Mr. V.A. Babu Charies Earnest | Member.

chunks.section: Placement Facilities — Infrastructure for Recruitment Activities
chunks.context: This chunk describes the physical and infrastructure facilities available at MSAJCE for conducting placement activities including written tests, group discussions, interviews and career counselling.
chunks.text: MSAJCE Placement Facilities: (1) Well-furnished air-conditioned rooms for written test, group discussion and face-to-face interview. (2) Well-established Computer labs for online training and examinations. (3) Air-conditioned Auditorium / Seminar Halls / Meeting Halls. (4) Dedicated AC rooms for conducting group discussions. (5) Career counselling is available on campus directing students to choose the best possible stream that meets their requirement in a unique way. The students have mentors who handle their problems and make them focused about their targets. Placement Details — PDF Downloads: Year-wise placement details are available for download: 2022–2023: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/placement/placementdetails/placementdetails-2022-2023.pdf; 2021–2022: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/placement/placementdetails/placementdetails-2021-2022.pdf; 2020–2021: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/placement/placementdetails/placementdetails-2020-2021.pdf; 2019–2020: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/placement/placementdetails/placementdetails-2019-2020.pdf; 2018–2019: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/placement/placementdetails/placementdetails-2018-2019.pdf; 2017–2018: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/placement/placementdetails/placementdetails-2017-2018.pdf; 2016–2017: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/placement/placementdetails/placementdetails-2016-2017.pdf. chunks.section: Industry Institution Interaction (III) Cell — Objectives, MoUs and Industries Visited
chunks.context: This chunk covers the III Cell objectives, complete list of 43 MoUs signed with industries from 2017 to 2023, and a selected list of industries visited by MSAJCE students.
chunks.text:
MSAJCE Industry Institution Interaction (III) Cell: Technical institutes must bridge the gap between industry and academia. In a competitive market due to increasing globalization of business, MSAJCE III Cell collaborates with industries to fulfil mutual needs. MSAJCE-III Cell helps students and faculty understand present industrial expectations and relevant skill sets, identifies skill gaps and provides upgrade opportunities, and enables faculty to train students to meet industrial needs.

III Cell Objectives: (1) Sign MoUs with industries for mutual benefit. (2) Organize workshops, conferences, seminars, guest lectures and symposia. (3) Encourage faculty to undergo industry training. (4) Engage industrial experts to interact with students. (5) Involve industrial experts in value-added course development. (6) Arrange industrial visits, internships and projects. (7) Encourage professional consultancy by faculty. (8) Arrange faculty development programs. (9) Organize placement drives. (10) Establish industry-oriented labs.

chunks.section: Higher Education Cell — Committee, Student Higher Education Details (2016–2023)
chunks.context: This chunk covers the Higher Education Cell objectives, committee members, and year-wise list of students who enrolled in higher education programmes from MSAJCE (2016–2023), including universities and programmes admitted to.
chunks.text:
MSAJCE Higher Education Cell: The Higher Education Committee regularly organizes meetings with successful alumni pursuing higher education and external agencies among interested students. It provides insight into different paths, risks and gains involved. Admissions counselling is provided to study-abroad aspirants to optimize their chances of obtaining admissions into the best universities jointly with external training providers.

Higher Education Cell Functions: (1) Create awareness among students on higher education in India and abroad. (2) Motivate students to reach their goal. (3) Realize student potential in the Higher Education sector. (4) Guide students to apply and appear for GATE, CAT, GRE and TOEFL examinations. (5) Inform students about programs related to Higher Education organized by other colleges.

Higher Education Committee:
